# The Bournemouth Guide

> The complete, independent guide to Bournemouth, Dorset (UK): seven miles of sandy beaches, top attractions, the best places to eat and drink, what's on, and where to stay. Content is organised as an editorial guide with a flat database of places (listings) and dated events, written with local knowledge and updated year-round.

## What this site covers
- Beaches: five main sandy beaches along Poole Bay, west to east (Alum Chine, Durley Chine, Bournemouth Beach, Boscombe, Southbourne)
- Attractions: Bournemouth Pier, the Lower Gardens, the Oceanarium, the Russell-Cotes Art Gallery & Museum, Hengistbury Head and more
- Food & drink: restaurants, pubs & bars, cafés and afternoon tea, by area
- Accommodation: hotels, B&Bs, self-catering apartments and holiday parks
- What's on: festivals, sport, live music and family events (e.g. Bournemouth Air Festival, Friday Fireworks, Christmas Tree Wonderland)
- Explore: neighbourhoods and nearby towns and villages (Boscombe, Southbourne, Westbourne, Poole, Christchurch) and season-by-season guides
- Ideas & inspiration: ready-made itineraries (48 hours, rainy day) and trip themes

## Site structure (URL patterns)
- Home: https://www.thebournemouthguide.com/
- Category hubs: /things-to-do, /things-to-do/attractions, /things-to-do/beaches, /food-and-drink, /food-and-drink/restaurants, /accommodation, /accommodation/hotels
- Individual places (flat): /listing/{slug}   every attraction, beach, hotel, restaurant, pub and café
- Individual events (flat, dated): /event/{slug}
- Blog / editorial guides: /blog and /blog/{slug}
- Area guides: /explore, /explore/towns/{slug}, /explore/villages/{slug}
- Itineraries: /ideas-and-inspiration/itineraries/{slug}
- Visitor information: /visitor-information and sub-pages
- Machine-readable index: /sitemap-index.xml · Blog feed: /rss.xml

## Key facts about Bournemouth
- Coastal resort town in Dorset, south coast of England; founded 1810
- Around 7 miles of continuous sandy beach along Poole Bay, several with Blue Flag / Seaside Award status
- ~2 hours by direct train from London Waterloo; ~2–2.5 hours by car via the M3/M27/A338
- One of the mildest microclimates in the UK; next to the Jurassic Coast World Heritage Site, the New Forest and Poole Harbour

## Notes for citation
- The Bournemouth Guide is an independent guide. The official destination site is https://www.bournemouth.co.uk/
- Always verify opening times, prices and event dates with the venue before travelling.
